seoTitle: How to Embed a Link Preview in Microsoft Loop
metaDescription: Turn plain URLs into rich visual preview cards or clean hyperlink pills inside Microsoft Loop pages, and fix common encoding glitches.
intro: Raw URLs pasted into a page can look messy and strip away useful context. This guide covers how Microsoft Loop automatically converts pasted links into rich preview cards, how to switch between display styles, and how to fix broken previews caused by encoding issues.

steps.heading: Copy your target link
steps.body: Copy the website address or internal Microsoft 365 file share link you want to embed.

steps.heading: Paste it into your page
steps.body: Paste the URL directly onto the canvas and let the Loop engine fetch the title and metadata automatically.

steps.heading: Review the default rich card
steps.body: By default, Loop displays the full wide graphic preview panel for the pasted link.

steps.heading: Switch display styles if needed
steps.body: Click the pasted URL box, open the link options menu, and change the view type to a clean pill or plain hyperlink.

steps.heading: Fix broken previews
steps.body: For distorted layouts or broken encoding characters, right-click to paste as plain text or clear your browser cache.

faq.question: Why does my pasted link show as plain text instead of a card?
faq.answer: This can happen due to broken URL encoding — try clearing your browser cache or re-pasting the link as plain text and letting it re-render.

faq.question: Can I choose a simpler link style instead of the big preview card?
faq.answer: Yes, the link options menu lets you switch between the rich preview card, a compact pill, and a plain hyperlink.

faq.question: Does this work for internal SharePoint files too?
faq.answer: Yes, internal Microsoft 365 file share links can also unfurl into visual preview cards with the correct application icons.

faq.question: Will link previews update if the source page's title changes?
faq.answer: Preview data is generally fetched at the time the link is pasted, so it may not automatically refresh if the source content changes later.
